No, not precisely yet; I left required parity gates, so it is a full path to parity but not an automatic guarantee of exact BSC equivalence.

Why it matters: “precisely like BSC” requires zero behavioral deltas, and missing even one edge rule in election/slashing/governance can change consensus or economics.

What should be done next:
1. Pin one exact BSC baseline (repo commit + rule set) as the only truth target.
2. Close every remaining delta with a strict pass/fail parity matrix (no “equivalent enough” items).
3. Run differential state-transition tests and claim exact parity only when the matrix is fully green with zero unresolved differences.
